title: Teyana Taylor's Golden Globes Upset Victory
text: Teyana Taylor claimed the Best Film Supporting Actress award at the 83rd Annual Golden Globes on January 11, 2026, for her role in One Battle After Another, defeating heavily favored Amy Madigan. The Surprise Win: Taylor's victory shocked both predictors and the actress herself. She revealed her disbelief during her acceptance speech, stating: I almost didn't even write a speech because I didn't think I would get this! According to Gold Derby's prediction data, Taylor carried only a 24% chance of winning, while Madigan held a commanding 64% advantage after winning the Critics Choice Award the previous week. Her Acceptance Remarks: The emotional winner thanked multiple people, including director Paul Thomas Anderson, telling him to let him cook while expressing gratitude for his vision and brilliance. She also delivered an inspiring message to young women of color in her closing remarks: Our softness is not a liability. Our light does not need permission to shine. We belong in every room we walk into. Performance Details: Despite having approximately 19 minutes of screen time as Perfidia Beverly Hills, Taylor's character left an indelible impact on the film, appearing opposite Leonardo DiCaprio and Sean Penn.
